 {"id":"bd-1eop","title":"Skip already-installed tools during install","description":"## Overview
Skip installation of tools that are already installed and at acceptable versions, making reinstalls and updates faster.

## Current Problem
- `acfs install` reinstalls everything
- Wastes time on already-installed tools
- No way to do incremental updates
- Full reinstall takes 5-10 minutes

## Proposed Behavior
- Check if tool exists in PATH
- Check if version meets minimum requirement
- Skip if both conditions met
- Show "✓ rust (already installed: 1.75.0)"

## Version Checking Strategy
```bash
check_tool_installed() {
  local tool="$1" min_version="$2"
  command -v "$tool" &>/dev/null || return 1
  local current=$("$tool" --version 2>/dev/null | grep -oE '[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1)
  version_gte "$current" "$min_version"
}
```

## Manifest Extension
```yaml
tools:
  rust:
    check_command: "rustc --version"
    min_version: "1.75.0"
    install_script: "https://sh.rustup.rs"
```

## Force Reinstall Flag
```bash
acfs install --force  # Reinstall everything
acfs install rust --force  # Reinstall specific tool
```

## Output Format
```
Checking installed tools...
✓ rust 1.79.0 (skipped)
✓ bun 1.1.38 (skipped)
⚡ zoxide (installing...)
✓ zoxide 0.9.4 (installed)

Installed 1 tool, skipped 2 (already up to date)
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test version comparison function
- [ ] Test skip detection for all tools
- [ ] Test --force overrides skip
- [ ] Test partial install (some skip, some install)
- [ ] Benchmark time savings

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/install_helpers.sh (add version checks)
- scripts/install.sh (skip logic)
- manifest.yaml (add version requirements)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:02:01.258391752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T04:07:53.308998788Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T04:07:53.308977898Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1eop","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:25:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1j4m","title":"Deep exploration: RU (Repo Updater)","description":"## Goal
Perform deep exploration of RU (Repo Updater) and revise its description on the flywheel/TLDR pages with comprehensive testing.

## Phase 0: Pre-flight Verification (CRITICAL)

### 0.1 Tool Existence Check
```bash
# Verify RU installation
[[ -d /dp/repo_updater ]] && echo "PASS: ru repo exists" || { echo "FAIL: ru repo missing"; exit 1; }
command -v ru &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: ru command available" || { echo "FAIL: ru not in PATH"; exit 1; }

# Check git availability (dependency)
command -v git &>/dev/null && echo "PASS: git available" || { echo "FAIL: git not installed"; exit 1; }
```

### 0.2 Content Snapshot (BEFORE State)
```bash
SNAPSHOT_DIR=/tmp/ru-exploration-snapshots-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
mkdir -p $SNAPSHOT_DIR
cp apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before
cp apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before
echo "Snapshots saved to $SNAPSHOT_DIR"
```

### 0.3 TypeScript Interface Reference
Content must match FlywheelTool interface:
- id, name, tagline, description, deepDescription
- features[], cliCommands[], connectsTo[]
- integrationLevel, category, status

## Phase 1: Research (GATHER CONTEXT)

### 1.1 Primary Documentation
- `cat /dp/repo_updater/README.md` - Read full README
- Check for repo configuration docs, batch operation specs

### 1.2 Code Investigation
- Launch code investigation agent to understand:
  - Repo discovery mechanism (/dp/ scanning)
  - Batch update operations (pull, fetch, status)
  - Conflict handling strategy
  - Parallel vs sequential processing
  - Status reporting format

### 1.3 CLI Command Verification
```bash
# Verify each documented command works
ru --help 2>&1 | head -20
ru status --help 2>&1 | head -10
ru pull --help 2>&1 | head -10

# Test actual functionality
ru status 2>&1 | head -10
```

### 1.4 External Context Search
- `/xf search 'repo updater OR ru OR batch git'` - Twitter archive
- `cass search 'ru repo update' --robot --limit 10` - Past sessions

### 1.5 Project State Review
- Check beads in /dp/repo_updater/.beads/
- Review recent commits: `cd /dp/repo_updater && git log --oneline -20`

## Phase 2: Analysis (SYNTHESIZE UNDERSTANDING)

### 2.1 Core Capabilities Verification
Document findings for each area:
- [ ] Repo discovery mechanism
- [ ] Batch operations available
- [ ] Conflict handling approach
- [ ] Parallel processing capability
- [ ] Status reporting format

### 2.2 Synergy Verification
Cross-reference these tools actually integrate:
- [ ] gh - GitHub CLI operations
- [ ] mail - status notifications
- [ ] ntm - coordinated updates

### 2.3 Performance Verification
```bash
# Count repos managed
ls -d /dp/*/.git 2>/dev/null | wc -l
echo "Total repos in /dp/"

# Test status speed
time ru status --quiet 2>/dev/null | head -5
```

## Phase 3: Revision (UPDATE DESCRIPTIONS)

### 3.1 Update apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
Update ru entry with VERIFIED information:
- `tagline`: Batch repo maintenance
- `description`: Update mechanism
- `deepDescription`: How batch operations work
- `features`: Verified capabilities
- `cliCommands`: Only commands that actually work
- `connectsTo`: Only verified integrations

### 3.2 Update apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
Update TldrFlywheelTool entry with:
- `briefDescription`: Technical summary
- `bulletPoints`: Verified capabilities
- `synergyExamples`: Working integration examples

## Phase 4: Testing (VERIFY CHANGES)

### 4.1 TypeScript Compilation
```bash
cd apps/web && npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | head -20
```

### 4.2 Unit Tests
```bash
# Test ru entry structure
node -e "
const { flywheelTools } = require('./lib/flywheel');
const ru = flywheelTools.find(t => t.id === 'ru');
console.log('Testing ru entry...');
console.assert(ru, 'ru entry exists');
console.assert(ru.features?.length > 0, 'has features');
console.assert(ru.cliCommands?.length > 0, 'has commands');
console.log('All assertions passed');
"
```

### 4.3 E2E Test: Batch Operations
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
LOG=/tmp/ru-e2e-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log

echo "=== RU E2E Test ===" | tee $LOG
echo "Started: $(date)" | tee -a $LOG

# Test 1: Status all repos
echo "Test 1: Status check..." | tee -a $LOG
ru status 2>&1 | head -15 | tee -a $LOG

# Test 2: List repos
echo "Test 2: List repos..." | tee -a $LOG
ru list 2>&1 | head -10 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No list command"

# Test 3: Dry run pull (if available)
echo "Test 3: Dry run..." | tee -a $LOG
ru pull --dry-run 2>&1 | head -5 | tee -a $LOG || echo "No dry-run option"

echo "=== All Tests Passed ===" | tee -a $LOG
echo "Log: $LOG"
```

### 4.4 Content Diff Verification
```bash
echo "=== Changes Made ==="
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts || true
diff $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts || true
```

## Phase 5: Completion (FINALIZE)

### 5.1 Rollback Procedure (if tests fail)
```bash
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/flywheel.ts.before apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts
cp $SNAPSHOT_DIR/tldr-content.ts.before apps/web/lib/tldr-content.ts
echo "Rolled back to pre-exploration state"
```

### 5.2 Sync Changes
```bash
br update bd-1j4m --status done
br sync --flush-only
```

### 5.3 Final Verification
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [ ] Batch operations documented correctly
- [ ] No broken links or references","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T20:01:32.106227619Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:45.634375752Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T03:32:45.634346477Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-1lug","title":"Rate limit backoff for GitHub API calls","description":"## Overview
Implement exponential backoff for GitHub API rate limit errors to prevent installation failures during peak times.

## Current Problem
- GitHub API has rate limits (60/hr unauthenticated, 5000/hr with token)
- Installation fetches many resources (releases, checksums)
- Rate limit errors cause hard failures
- No retry mechanism

## Proposed Behavior
- Detect 403 rate limit responses
- Implement exponential backoff: 1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, max 60s
- Show user-friendly message during wait
- Fall back to cached versions if available
- Suggest `gh auth login` if unauthenticated

## Implementation Details
```bash
github_fetch_with_backoff() {
  local url="$1" max_retries=5 delay=1
  for ((i=1; i<=max_retries; i++)); do
    response=$(curl -sS -w '%{http_code}' -o /tmp/response "$url")
    if [[ "$response" == "200" ]]; then
      cat /tmp/response; return 0
    elif [[ "$response" == "403" ]]; then
      log_warn "Rate limited, waiting ${delay}s (attempt $i/$max_retries)"
      sleep "$delay"
      delay=$((delay * 2))
    else
      return 1
    fi
  done
  return 1
}
```

## Rate Limit Detection
- HTTP 403 with "rate limit" in body
- X-RateLimit-Remaining header = 0
- X-RateLimit-Reset header for exact wait time

## User Communication
```
⏳ GitHub rate limit reached. Waiting 8s before retry (3/5)...
💡 Tip: Run 'gh auth login' for higher rate limits
```

## Test Plan
- [ ] Test backoff timing is correct
- [ ] Test max retries respected
- [ ] Test successful retry after limit clears
- [ ] Mock test for rate limit response handling
- [ ] Test fallback to cached versions

## Files to Modify
- scripts/lib/security.sh (fetch_with_checksum uses curl)
- New: scripts/lib/github_api.sh (GitHub-specific helpers)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T23:01:55.353096702Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T02:15:03.879490198Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T02:15:03.879471172Z","close_reason":"Implemented github_api.sh with exponential backoff for rate limits. Integrated into security.sh. Added unit tests (12/12 passing). Features: HTTP 403/429 detection, X-RateLimit-Remaining header support, user-friendly wait messages with gh auth tip, configurable backoff (1s-60s).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1lug","depends_on_id":"bd-3y1n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:25:53Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
